topic: oxidative ageing and tertiary aroma development

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Wine is deliberately matured with controlled access to oxygen.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Oxygen-driven reactions transform primary fruit compounds and promote the development of mature aroma and flavour compounds.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Fresh primary fruit becomes less dominant and tertiary characteristics such as nuts, dried fruit, caramel, or savoury notes can develop according to wine style.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Fresh primary fruit becomes less dominant and tertiary characteristics such as nuts, dried fruit, caramel, or savoury notes can develop according to wine style.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
